```cpp
}
```

```cpp
Result<Ref<Connection>> write(const Ref<Connection>& _conn, ItBegin _begin,
                              ItEnd _end) noexcept {
  using T =

  const auto start_write = [&](const auto&) -> Result<Nothing> {

    return _conn->start_write(write_stmt);
  };

  const auto write = [&](const auto&) -> Result<Nothing> {
    const auto res = _conn->write(_begin, _end);
    if (!res) {
      _conn->end_write();
    }
    return res;
  };

  const auto end_write = [&](const auto&) -> Result<Nothing> {
    return _conn->end_write();
  };

  const auto create_table_stmt = transpilation::to_create_table<T>();

  return _conn->execute(_conn->to_sql(create_table_stmt))
      .and_then(start_write)
      .and_then(write)
      .and_then(end_write)

}
```

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace query_search_books_by_criteria {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> book_id;
  std::string title;
  std::string author;
  int year;
  std::string genre;
  double price;
};

struct BookFilters {
  std::optional<std::string> title_keyword;
  std::optional<int> min_year;
  std::optional<std::string> genre;
  std::optional<double> max_price;
};

/**
 * Function: Search for books based on multiple optional criteria.
 * 
 * @param filters Optional filters for title, year, genre, and price
 * @return List of books matching the criteria
 */
std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& filters) {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;

    // Connect to the database
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

    // Build the condition
    auto condition = true;

    if (filters.title_keyword) {
        condition = condition && ("title"_c.like("%" + *filters.title_keyword + "%"));
    }
    if (filters.min_year) {
        condition = condition && ("year"_c >= *filters.min_year);
    }
    if (filters.genre) {
        condition = condition && ("genre"_c == *filters.genre);
    }
    if (filters.max_price) {
        condition = condition && ("price"_c <= *filters.max_price);
    }

    // Build the query
    auto query = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Book>> | sqlgen::where(condition);

    // Execute the query and return the result
    return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace query_search_books_by_criteria
```

One more thing: the `JoinResult` struct has optional fields. The joins may return NULL values for some fields, so we use `std::optional` to represent them.

The `read` function will automatically map the results to the `JoinResult` struct, and the NULL values will be represented as `std::nullopt`.

We are ready to write the code.

### Final Code:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace analyze_department_employees {

struct Department {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
  std::string department_name;
  std::string location;
};

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> employee_id;
  int department_id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
  double salary;
};

struct JoinResult {
  std::optional<std::string> department_name;
  std::optional<std::string> first_name;
  std::optional<std::string> last_name;
  std::optional<double> salary;
};

struct JoinAnalysis {
  std::vector<JoinResult> inner_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> left_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> right_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> full_join_results;
};

JoinAnalysis analyze_department_employees_impl(const std::vector<Department>& depts, const std::vector<Employee>& emps) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(":memory:");

  // Create tables
  auto create_dept = sqlgen::create_table<Department>;
  auto create_emp = sqlgen::create_table<Employee>;

  // Insert data
  auto insert_dept = sqlgen::insert(depts);
  auto insert_emp = sqlgen::insert(emps);

  // Execute the operations on the connection
  conn | create_dept;
  conn | create_emp;
  conn | insert_dept;
  conn | insert_emp;

  // INNER JOIN
  auto inner_query = sqlgen::select_from<Department, "t1">("department_name"_t1, "first_name"_t2, "last_name"_t2, "salary"_t2)
      | sqlgen::inner_join<Employee, "t2">("department_id"_t1 == "department_id"_t2);
  auto inner_results = sqlgen::read<std::vector<JoinResult>>(inner_query)(conn).value();

  // LEFT JOIN
  auto left_query = sqlgen::select_from<Employee, "t2">("first_name"_t2, "last_name"_t2, "salary"_t2, "department_name"_t1)
      | sqlgen::left_join<Department, "t1">("department_id"_t2 == "department_id"_t1);
  auto left_results = sqlgen::read<std::vector<JoinResult>>(left_query)(conn).value();

  // RIGHT JOIN
  auto right_query = sqlgen::select_from<Department, "t1">("department_name"_t1, "first_name"_t2, "last_name"_t2, "salary"_t2)
      | sqlgen::right_join<Employee, "t2">("department_id"_t1 == "department_id"_t2);
  auto right_results = sqlgen::read<std::vector<JoinResult>>(right_query)(conn).value();

  // FULL JOIN
  auto full_query = sqlgen::select_from<Department, "t1">("department_name"_t1, "first_name"_t2, "last_name"_t2, "salary"_t2)
      | sqlgen::full_join<Employee, "t2">("department_id"_t1 == "department_id"_t2);
  auto full_results = sqlgen::read<std::vector<JoinResult>>(full_query)(conn).value();

  // Build the JoinAnalysis struct
  JoinAnalysis analysis;
  analysis.inner_join_results = inner_results;
  analysis.left_join_results = left_results;
  analysis.right_join_results = right_results;
  analysis.full_join_results = full_results;

  return analysis;
}

}  // namespace analyze_department_employees
```

This code should satisfy the requirements of the exam.

Note: We are using the `value()` method to extract the vector from the `Result` returned by the `read` function. If the operation fails, this will throw an exception. Since the problem does not specify error handling, we assume that the operations succeed.

If we are to handle errors, we would need to change the function signature to return a `Result<JoinAnalysis>`. But the problem says to return `JoinAnalysis`.

We have followed the specifications and used the provided functions and global variables.

      .from_ = transpilation::TableWrapper<TableType>{}};
}

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace project_time_tracking {

using namespace sqlgen::literals;

struct Project {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct TimeLog {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> log_id;
  int project_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> end_time;
};

struct TimeMetrics {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
  std::string name;
  int day;
  int month;
  int year;
  int hour;
  int minute;
  int second;
  double duration_days;
};

std::vector<TimeMetrics> project_time_tracking() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Project, TimeLog>(
      "name"_t1 | sqlgen::as<"name">,
      "project_id"_t1 | sqlgen::as<"project_id">,
      sqlgen::day("start_time"_t2) | sqlgen::as<"day">,
      sqlgen::month("start_time"_t2) | sqlgen::as<"month">,
      sqlgen::year("start_time"_t2) | sqlgen::as<"year">,
      sqlgen::hour("start_time"_t2) | sqlgen::as<"hour">,
      sqlgen::minute("start_time"_t2) | sqlgen::as<"minute">,
      sqlgen::second("start_time"_t2) | sqlgen::as<"second">,
      sqlgen::sum(sqlgen::days_between("start_time"_t2, "end_time"_t2)) | sqlgen::as<"duration_days">
  )
  | sqlgen::inner_join<TimeLog>("project_id"_t1 == "project_id"_t2)
  | sqlgen::group_by("project_id"_t1, sqlgen::week("start_time"_t2), 
                     "day"_t2, "month"_t2, "year"_t2, "hour"_t2, "minute"_t2, "second"_t2)
  | sqlgen::to<std::vector<TimeMetrics>>;
  return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace project_time_tracking
```
